latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 17:09
RNS Number : 0346K
Devolver Digital, Inc.
26 June 2026
26 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Result of AGM
Devolver Digital (AIM: DEVO), an award-winning digital publisher and developer of indie video games, is pleased to announce that
all resolutions proposed at the Annual Meeting of the Company held on Friday 26 June 2026 were duly passed.
The full text of the resolutions can be found in the Notice of Annual Meeting, which is available on the Company's website at
https://investors.devolverdigital.com
The full proxy results will be available shortly on the Company's website at
https://investors.devolverdigital.com
.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8529J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
26 June 2026
26 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Trading Statement
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games,
provides an update on current financial performance for the half year ending 30 June 2026 ("1H 2026") and outlook for full year 2026.
1H 2026 performance has been ahead of expectations with a very strong revenue performance expected to be at least 60% higher than 1H 2025, with underlying Adjusted EBITDA projected to reach mid single-digit US$ millions. The favourable year-on-year comparison is in part due to the Devolver Steam Publisher sale being held in 1H 2026, in addition to a positive start to the year with 3 Top 10 Global Best Sellers on Steam in January.
In June, Devolver's subsidiary System Era released
STARSEEKER: Astroneer Expeditions
into Early Access with a significant platform deal accompanying the launch. Unit sales have been disappointing so far but review scores are improving and there are regular content updates planned for August and through to the end of 2026 which the Board believes can re-energise the game. Separately, we are encouraged by our strong lineup in the second half of the year. Subsidiary studio Firefly enjoyed a very positive response to its announcement of
Stronghold 4,
building 250,000 wishlists in just two weeks. Equally encouraging is the interest in other games slated for 2H 2026,
Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun 2
(c. 260,000 wishlists) and
Shroom and Gloom
(c. 220,000 wishlists).
The strong 1H 2026 performance and the positive response to pending games are expected to combine to build a profitable cushion although this will be offset by the slower than expected initial launch of
STARSEEKER
. We will provide further details on 1H 2026 performance and guidance for the full year 2026 at the time of the Company's half-year results in late September 2026.
The 2026 Annual Meeting will be held this afternoon at 4:00pm BST and results of the meeting will be issued upon its conclusion. The 2025 Annual report and Accounts are available on, and can be downloaded from, Devolver Digital's investor website at investors.devolverdigital.com.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

latest.title: TR-1: Notification of Major Holdings
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 12:15
RNS Number : 8047J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Holding(s) in Company
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("
indie
") video games, has been notified of the following change in the interests of David Darling and Kwalee Ltd in the shares of the Company.
On 24 June 2026, 16,758,575 shares of common stock of $0.0001 par value each ("
shares
") in the Company were transferred from Kwalee Ltd to David Darling. The transfer represents a reclassification of shares between connected parties and does not result in any change to the aggregate number of shares in which David Darling is interested. David Darling is the majority shareholder of Kwalee Ltd.
Following the transfer, David Darling's total beneficial interest in the Company is unchanged at 23,588,575 representing 4.86% of the total issued share capital of the Company.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 0914H
Devolver Digital, Inc.
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company", and the Company together with all of its subsidiary undertakings "the Group")
Notice of Annual Meeting
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games, announces that its Notice of Annual Meeting ("AM") has been published.
The Notice of AM is available on, and can be downloaded from, the Company's investor website at: https://investors.devolverdigital.com and will be posted to shareholders who request hard copies.
The AM of the Company will be held at Fieldfisher's offices, 9th Floor, Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London EC4R 3TT, United Kingdom on 26 June 2026 at 4:00 p.m. (UK time).
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 145 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next few years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

company_30s.what_they_do: Devolver Digital makes and publishes indie video games. They own a catalog of cult titles, run a publishing arm that helps small studios reach gamers, and run a community‑focused platform that streams and promotes new releases.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Indie games are a growing niche that often out‑performs big‑budget titles in profitability. Devolver has a strong brand, a loyal fan base, and a proven track record of turning low‑budget projects into cult hits. Their catalogue gives them a steady revenue stream while their publishing arm keeps them in touch with the next wave of creative talent.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just finished a quarterly earnings report that beat revenue expectations and announced a few new titles slated for release. The market reaction was muted, and the stock has slid sharply on weak volume. It sits well below its historical highs, but the fundamentals – a solid pipeline and a loyal fan base – suggest it could rebound once momentum picks up.

rns_narrative: The first cluster shows the company’s governance updates – an AGM result, a major holdings notification, and a board change – all of which signal stability and confidence from insiders.

The second cluster highlights the financial side: a preliminary results release that beat revenue estimates and a trading statement that gives a clearer view of cash flow. This suggests the business is staying afloat and may be tightening its budget.
The third cluster is about product momentum – announcements of new game releases and updates to the publishing pipeline. These are the catalysts that could drive future sales and lift the stock.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a fresh game slate
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders point to the revenue beat and upcoming titles as evidence that Devolver’s creative pipeline is healthy and that the market is under‑reacting to a solid business model.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the game library is aging, that competition from larger studios is eroding margins, and that the company’s focus on indie titles may limit growth.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: After the earnings release, the chatter has moved from neutral to mildly bullish, but the volume remains low, indicating that many traders are still waiting for a clear signal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there’s almost no public search interest, which means any new information could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report, scheduled for early July, could confirm whether the revenue beat is repeatable.
The launch of the next big indie title in mid‑July, which has already generated pre‑orders, might spark a sales spike.
A potential partnership announcement with a major platform (e.g., Steam, Xbox) that would expand distribution could add credibility.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds 1.5× the 50‑day average, a breakout above the last support level, and any analyst upgrade. These signals would confirm that the market is starting to price in the upside.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a capitulation cycle – it’s still falling but the daily volume has thinned. A breakout that aligns with a product release or earnings confirmation would be the trigger for mean‑reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Devolver sits deep below its recent highs, giving a clear upside if it returns to the 50% pre‑crash level. A deep crash often produces a large rebound, and the company’s recent earnings beat and fresh game pipeline provide a strong narrative for a turnaround. Historically, the system’s RED and PURPLE signals (deep crashes with institutional accumulation) have delivered over 150% returns, so this setup has the potential for a 2–3× payoff.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the indie game market is highly competitive and that the new titles may not hit the expected sales. If the next release underperforms or if the company runs into cash‑flow issues, the stock could stay near its low and fail to recover. A realistic downside is the current level, which would mean a 70–80% loss from the recent peak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 70% of the current price. Potential gain: 200–300% if the stock climbs back to 50–70% of its pre‑crash level. The upside far outweighs the downside, making this a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price clears the last support level with at least 1.5× average daily volume. Size the position so that a 15% drop from entry would trigger a stop. This keeps the downside capped while letting you benefit from a large rebound.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until either the earnings report confirms repeatable growth or the new title launches and shows strong pre‑orders. A price break above the 30‑day moving average with volume > 2× average would be a clear entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None. No delisting or bankruptcy signals are present; the only real risk is a failure to deliver on the game pipeline.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Devolver sits in a deep crash with a quiet market, and the upcoming earnings and new game releases could spark a sharp rebound, offering a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian opportunity.
